mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases or when industrial production growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally with positive returns in > 65% of cases when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect expanding economic output and corporate revenue bases, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth changes with Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and earnings downgrade risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 60% of instances when industrial production falls below the threshold.
